502 तक का अंतर हो सकेगा । 1/100वां जमा या घटा अथा榁त वजन 34.65 灡ाम से 35.35 灡ाम के बीच हो सकता है । पांच 牱पये 6 灡ाम तांबा और ज瀡ता के िलए 1/100वां िह瀡सा कम या अिधक अथा榁त तांबा 74 灹ितशत से 76 灹ितशत 1/40वां िह瀡सा कम या अिधक अथा榁त वजन 5.85 灡ाम से 6.15 灡ाम के बीच हो सकता -- 2 of 4 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 3 और ज瀡ता म犆 19 灹ितशत से 21 灹ितशत तक हो सकता है तथा िनकल के िलए 1/200वां िह瀡सा कम या अिधक अथा榁त िनकल 4.5 灹ितशत से 5.5 灹ितशत तक हो सकता है। है । [फा. सं. 02/02/2017-िस猳ा] ईि瀉सता िम灴ा, अवर सिचव MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Economic Affairs) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th June, 2018 G.S.R. 592(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by clauses (d) and (e) of sub-section (2) of section 24 of the Coinage Act, 2011 (11 of 2011), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely:— 1. Short title and commencement.—(1) These rules may be called the Coinage (Issue of Commemorative coins on the occasion of 125th Birth Anniversary of Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis) Rules, 2018. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Denomination of Coins.—The coins of the following denominations shall also be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of 125th Birth Anniversary of Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis, namely:— (1) One Hundred Twenty Five Rupees; (2) Five Rupees 3. Dimension and composition of coins.—The coins of the above denominations mentioned in rule 2 shall conform to the following dimensions and composition, namely:— Denomination of the coin Shape and outside diameter Number of Serrations Metal Composition (1) (2) (3) (4) One Hundred Twenty Five Rupees Circular 44 millimeter 200 Quaternary Alloy Silver - 50 Per cent Copper - 40 Per cent Nickel - 05 Per cent Zinc - 05 Per cent Five Rupees Circular 23 millimeter 100 Nickel Brass Copper - 75 Per cent Zinc - 20 Per cent Nickel - 05 Per cent 4. Design.—The design of the coins shall be as specified in the First Schedule to these rules. 5. Standard weight and remedy allowed.—The standard weight of the coins of One Hundred Twenty Five Rupees and Five Rupees coined to commemorate the occasion of 125th Birth Anniversary of Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis and the remedy allowed in making of such coins shall be as specified in the Second Schedule to these rules. First Schedule (See rule 4) (i) ONE HUNDRED TWENTY FIVE RUPEES OBVERSE: This face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “स瀄यमेव जयते” inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word “ भारत ” in Devnagari script and on the right periphery with the word “INDIA” in English and it shall also bear the Rupee symbol “`” and denominational value “125” in International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. -- 3 of 4 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] REVERSE: This face of the coin shall bear the portrait of “PRASANTA CHANDRA MAHALANOBIS” in the centre with the inscription “125व牷 जयंती” and “灹शांत चं灶 महालनोिबस” on upper left and upper right periphery of the coin in Devnagari script and the inscription “125th BIRTH ANNIVERSARY” and “PRASANTA CHANDRA MAHALANOBIS” shall be on the Lower left and lower right periphery of the coin respectively. The year 1893-1972 in International numerals shall be written below the portrait of “Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis” and the symbol of “Indian Statistical Institute” shall be inscribed below the year 1893-1972. (ii) FIVE RUPEES OBVERSE: This face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “स瀄यमेव जयते” inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word “ भारत ” in Devnagari script and on the right periphery with the word “INDIA” in English. It shall also bear the Rupee symbol “`” and denominational value “5” in International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. REVERSE: This face of the coin shall bear the portrait of “PRASANTA CHANDRA MAHALANOBIS” in the Centre with the inscription “125व牷 जयंती” and “灹शांत चं灶 महालनोिबस” on upper left and upper right periphery of the coin in Devnagari script. The inscription “125th BIRTH ANNIVERSARY” and “PRASANTA CHANDRA MAHALANOBIS” shall be on the Lower left and lower right periphery of the coin respectively. The year 1893-1972 in International numerals shall be written below the portrait of “Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis” and the symbol of “Indian Statistical Institute” shall be inscribed below the year 1893-1972. Second Schedule (See Rule 5) Denomination of coin Standard Weight Remedy Allowed In composition In Standard Weight (1) (2) (3) (4) One Hundred Twenty Five Rupees 35 Grams 1/500th plus or minus for silver, that is to say the Silver content may vary from 498 to 502 per 1000. 1/100th plus or minus that is to say weight could vary from 34.65 grams to 35.35 grams. Five Rupees 6 Grams 1/100th plus or minus for Copper and Zinc, that is to say Copper could vary from 74 per cent to 76 per cent and Zinc from 19 per cent to 21 per cent and 1/200th plus and minus for Nickel that is to say Nickel could vary from 4.5 per cent to 5.5 per cent. 1/40th plus or minus, that is to say, the weight could vary from 5.85 grams to 6.15 grams. [F. No. 02/02/2017-Coin] IPSITA MITRA, Under Secy.
